With only 5 distinct geo markets and a directionality of 0.89, the trader bets heavily in one direction within their specialist domains. The 'Other' category is not noise — 491 losses suggests active misallocation into unfamiliar territory.
domain_blindspots: Other: catastrophic 6.5% accuracy across 491 trades implies systematic misjudgement outside core domains, possibly stubborn directional bias applied in wrong contexts
domain_summary: This trader has a genuine and exceptional edge in Russia-Ukraine markets (best-in-class 91.5%) and a meaningful edge in Trump/US political markets. Outside these two domains, their signal is worse than random and should be treated as anti-signal.
signal_weight_reasoning: Russia_UKR and Trump_US signals deserve FULL weight given the sample size and accuracy. However, 'Other' trades are so consistently wrong (6.5%) that following them would be actively harmful. DOMAIN_ONLY weighting correctly captures the bifurcated performance profile.

summary: This is a LEGENDARY-tier specialist with one of the strongest Russia-Ukraine track records observable in the dataset (91.5% over ~1,062 trades), complemented by solid Trump_US performance. The core edge is real, large-sample, and worth treating as a high-quality signal within those domains. However, outside Russia_UKR and Trump_US, this trader is systematically wrong — 6.5% accuracy in 'Other' is worse than random chance and likely reflects directional bias misapplied to unfamiliar contexts. The recent Harris market debacle (15 consecutive losses, entries at ~0.69) illustrates a risk of sunk-cost escalation when conviction is wrong. Use this trader's Russia_UKR and Trump_US signals at full weight; treat all other trades as anti-signal.
